assertion_results.text: Step 2.3.5 dependency chain context identifies criterion as a dev-only dependency based on its presence in [dev-dependencies] in the manifest
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Step 2.3.5 states 'Profile: dev-only ([dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml)' and 'NOT present in production builds -- used for benchmarks only'. Manifest evidence section shows '[dev-dependencies] criterion = "0.5.1"' from backend/Cargo.toml. The Dependency Scope Assessment further confirms: 'criterion is declared in [dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml. Dev-only dependencies are NOT shipped in production builds -- they are used for benchmarks and tests only.'

assertion_results.text: The dependency scope decision tree classifies criterion as 'dev-only — not shipped in production' and applies the dev-dependency remediation handling
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 54-60 explicitly reference the decision tree: 'Per the dependency scope decision tree: criterion is a dev-only dependency and is not shipped in production. Still create remediation tasks (supply chain risk), but with modifications: Add the dev-dependency label, Set priority to Normal regardless of CVE severity, Include a note about dev/build-only scope.' remediation.md line 158 reiterates: 'Per the dependency scope decision tree in version-impact-analysis.md: Dev-only dependencies still represent a supply chain risk.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include the dev-dependency label in the labels array: labels contain 'dev-dependency' alongside 'ai-generated-jira', 'Security', and the CVE ID
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 labels: 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-99001, dev-dependency'. Task 2 labels: 'ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-99001, dev-dependency'. Jira Operations Summary confirms both tasks use labels '["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-99001", "dev-dependency"]'. All four required labels are present in both remediation tasks.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task priority is set to Normal regardless of the CVE severity (CVSS 5.3 Medium) — the priority override is applied per the decision tree
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1: 'Priority: Normal'. Task 2: 'Priority: Normal'. The Dev-Dependency Priority Override Rationale section states: 'Priority is set to Normal regardless of CVE severity (CVSS 5.3 Medium)'. version-impact.md also states: 'Set priority to Normal regardless of CVE severity (CVSS 5.3 Medium)'. The override is explicitly attributed to the decision tree for dev-only dependencies.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description includes a note indicating the dependency is dev/build-only and not shipped in production
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 description contains: 'This dependency is dev/build-only and is not shipped in production. Remediation priority is Normal (supply chain risk only).' and 'Dependency scope: dev-only ([dev-dependencies] in backend/Cargo.toml) -- NOT shipped in production builds, used for benchmarks only'. Task 2 description contains: 'This dependency is dev/build-only and is not shipped in production. Remediation priority is Normal (supply chain risk only).' and 'Dependency scope: dev-only -- NOT shipped in production'.
